name: lookup
summary: Allows to define lookup maps. They are used for defining custom coloring of maps. (see colour command.)
syntax: lookup <type>[:<index>] [-title "custom title text"] <parameter(s)> [colour] ["text in legend"] ... endlookup

arguments.raw: <type> = May be altitude, explo-date, topo-date, map or scrap.
arguments.name: <type>
arguments.description: May be altitude, explo-date, topo-date, map or scrap.

arguments.raw: <index> = the index is a user defined string that allows to distinguish different lookup maps for the same type.
arguments.name: <index>
arguments.description: the index is a user defined string that allows to distinguish different lookup maps for the same type.

arguments.raw: <parameter> = the thing, you want to set the color for, e.g. an altitude with type altitude. May be distinct values or (if appropriate) bands.
arguments.name: <parameter>
arguments.description: the thing, you want to set the color for, e.g. an altitude with type altitude. May be distinct values or (if appropriate) bands.

arguments.raw: [colour] = grayscale value, RGB triplet, CMYK quadruplet or a combination of eight values (RGB, grayscale, CMYK). Use empty brackets ([]) if you want Therion to use it’s default colour palette together with a text label.
arguments.name: [colour]
arguments.description: grayscale value, RGB triplet, CMYK quadruplet or a combination of eight values (RGB, grayscale, CMYK). Use empty brackets ([]) if you want Therion to use it’s default colour palette together with a text label.

arguments.raw: [text in legend] = some optional text to show in the legend.
arguments.name: [text in legend]
arguments.description: some optional text to show in the legend.
